title: dalbergia retusa
intro: Dalbergia retusa, also known as the Philippine rosewood or Molave, is a species of tree native to the Philippines and parts of Southeast Asia. It is valued for its timber and medicinal properties, with extracts from its bark and roots having anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and relieving digestive issues due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds. In traditional medicine, Dalbergia retusa is prepared as a decoction or infusion, often combined with other herbs, to treat various ailments, and its wood is also used in herbal preparations like teas and infusions.
